title: theme — declarative attributes
description: One PHP attribute — #[AsThemeConsumer] — that a downstream module uses to mark itself as a theme-token consumer. Discovery at boot compiles a list of consumers used for cache warming + fanout on ThemeCacheInvalidated.

attributes.name: AsThemeConsumer
attributes.purpose: Marks a class as a theme-token consumer. Registers with the ThemeConsumerRegistry so downstream cache-warming + ThemeCacheInvalidated fanout know to bust the consumer's own caches. Applied to notification templates, email renderers, PDF invoice renderers, SDUI screen resolvers.

arguments.name: concern
arguments.description: Short identifier of what the consumer renders — 'email-template', 'pdf-invoice', 'sdui-screen', 'og-image'. Used for observability grouping.

arguments.name: consumesModes
arguments.description: Which theme modes the consumer renders. Email templates typically ['light']; SDUI screens typically both.

arguments.name: invalidatesCacheKey
arguments.description: Optional consumer-owned cache key to purge on ThemeCacheInvalidated. Null = the consumer handles invalidation internally via its own event listener.

conventions: Consumer registration is OPTIONAL — a module can read theme tokens via TokenResolver without registering. The attribute exists for lifecycle-integrated consumers (email templates + PDF renderers) that need cache invalidation on theme change.
Every #[AsThemeConsumer] class is compiled into a ThemeConsumerRegistry at boot via foundation's DiscoversAttributes seam.
The registry is inspected by ThemeCacheInvalidated fanout to selectively purge downstream caches.
